title: Wall Street banks consolidate as the new engine of artificial intelligence
summary: Morgan Stanley, Citigroup and other large banks post solid quarterly results, confirming the thesis that the sector has become a key player in the AI boom.

title: Wall Street closes higher on inflation moderation and Fed pause expectations
summary: The Dow Jones, S&P 500 and Nasdaq rise after lower-than-expected CPI and PPI, while the Fed's Beige Book confirms a general moderation in prices.

title: SpaceX plummets: shares fall below IPO price and bonds approach 'junk' status
summary: Elon Musk's space company loses more than a trillion dollars in market valuation amid investor panic over lack of confidence in the business.

title: Fed, JPMorgan and Microsoft issue coordinated warnings about a possible AI investment bubble
summary: Kevin Warsh, Jamie Dimon and Microsoft's CEO warn that capital could dry up if AI companies fail to meet market expectations.

title: Asian stocks rise sharply driven by optimism on Wall Street and Fed easing
summary: The Nikkei, KOSPI, CSI 300 and markets in Hong Kong, Taiwan and Singapore post broad gains thanks to improved global risk appetite.

title: Stripe and Advent launch a $53 billion takeover bid for PayPal
summary: The news sends PayPal shares up more than 16% on Wall Street and sparks speculation about the future of the fintech sector and acquisitions.

title: ASML raises sales forecast to €45 billion and beats profit records
summary: The Dutch chipmaker earns 27% more in the second quarter, driven by unstoppable demand for its lithography equipment for artificial intelligence.

title: US attacks Iranian oil tanker and raises tensions in the Strait of Hormuz
summary: Trump returns to the threat of the 30-day memorandum while Iran says it will not negotiate, and oil markets prepare for a new global supply shock.

title: Global tech sell-off: IBM, Pentair and Dell drag down semiconductor markets
summary: The collapse of US tech giants weighs on the Nikkei and S&P 500, in a day of strong sector rotation and caution ahead of earnings season.

title: BlackRock reaches a historic record of $15.3 trillion in assets under management
summary: The world's largest asset manager exceeds the $15 trillion mark thanks to the strong recovery of global stock markets in the first half.

title: Apple reaches a new all-time high on the stock market and leads Dow Jones gains
summary: The tech giant's shares rise more than 4%, reaffirming Warren Buffett's bet on the company amid the artificial intelligence boom.

title: Chinese chipmaker CXMT seeks a $10 billion IPO, the largest since 2010
summary: The memory company aims to list on the stock exchange to finance its expansion amid growing demand for semiconductors and geopolitical tensions.

title: Nvidia invests $2 billion in an alternative to its NVLink standard and strengthens its alliance with Toyota
summary: The company backs a chip interconnection startup and provides physical AI technology for the Woven City smart city.

title: Johnson & Johnson, Elevance Health and M&T Bank post solid second-quarter results
summary: The US healthcare and banking companies beat market expectations in the corporate earnings season in the United States.

title: ECB points to a pause in rates while EU accelerates banking sector deregulation
summary: Joachim Nagel considers a pause appropriate in July, while Brussels pushes measures to ease financial rules and foster competitiveness in Europe.

title: Alcoa reports results and BHP recovers iron ore production in a mixed demand environment
summary: Mining companies offer a contrasting view of the commodities sector, with copper production still subdued and iron ore showing signs of recovery.

title: Warren Buffett excludes the Gates Foundation from his annual donation and allocates $4.5 billion to his children
summary: It is the first time since 2006 that the investor has not donated to the Bill Gates foundation, redirecting funds to his own family charitable works.

title: Ibex 35 seeks catalysts to surpass 20,000 points after the recent boost from the World Cup
summary: The Spanish benchmark takes a breather in its rally, awaiting new economic and corporate catalysts to consolidate the upward trend.

title: Japanese scientist Tonegawa Susumu, winner of the 1987 Nobel Prize in Medicine, passes away
summary: The immunologist, a pioneer in the study of the genetic diversity of antibodies, dies at 86 leaving an indelible scientific legacy in the world.

title: German Dax closes in the red and falls just short of the 25,000-point mark
summary: Frankfurt's main stock index halts its bull run amid doubts about the global economy and the future of interest rate policies.
